message:
fix: prevent double slot release, window-based debt decay, and align test comments to actual weights

Three correctness and maintainability improvements across the
cancellation orchestrator and reliability model.

--- cancellation.rs: double-cancellation guard ---

Add an atomic check-and-insert in `cancel_build()` so that if a
concurrent caller requests cancellation of the same build_id while
one is already in progress, the second call returns immediately with
the existing record's state instead of inserting a duplicate. Without
this guard, two concurrent cancellations would both attempt to release
the same worker slots, effectively stealing slots from a subsequent
build.

--- cancellation.rs: conditional slot release on completion ---

In the completion handler, slots are now only released when
`cancel_active_build()` successfully moved the record out of the
active map (i.e. `history_ok` is true). If the build already completed
normally via `handle_release_worker`, that path already released the
slots; releasing again here would double-release.

--- cancellation.rs: window-based debt counters ---

Replace the monotonically-increasing `escalation_total: u32` and
`cleanup_failures: u32` scalar counters with timestamped vectors
(`recent_escalations: Vec<Instant>`, `recent_cleanup_failures:
Vec<Instant>`). These are pruned to DEBT_WINDOW in
`cancellation_debt()` alongside `recent_cancellations`, so that a
worker's debt naturally decays as old events age out instead of
permanently accumulating.

The `record_cancellation()` method now pushes N timestamps for N
escalations (one per escalation step) and a single timestamp for
cleanup failure, matching the windowed model.

The `cancellation_debt()` computation is updated to use `.len()` on
the pruned vectors instead of the old totals. Semantics and weights
are otherwise unchanged.

Test updates in `cancellation.rs`:
- `test_cancellation_debt_weighted_formula`: populate timestamped
  vectors instead of setting scalar fields.
- `test_cancellation_debt_capped_at_one`: same migration to vectors
  with 100 entries each.
- Remove unused `WorkerConfig` import.

--- reliability.rs: fix stale weight comments in tests ---

The reliability weight for circuit_debt was evidently changed from 0.35
to 0.30 (and convergence_debt from 0.25 to 0.22) in production code at
some point, but the inline comments in 9 test functions still referenced
the old 0.35/0.25 weights. Update all affected arithmetic comments to
reflect the actual 0.30/0.22 weights and recomputed thresholds:

- `test_quarantine_on_high_circuit_debt`: 0.35 -> 0.30, threshold note.
- `test_multi_signal_quarantine_with_convergence`: 0.35+0.25 -> 0.30+0.22.
- `test_quarantine_recovery_via_successes`: 0.35 -> 0.30, debt 0.007 -> 0.006.
- `test_reentry_penalty_after_quarantine_recovery`: same.
- `test_quarantine_backoff_escalation`: same.
- `test_circuit_degraded_state`: 0.35 -> 0.30.
- `test_degraded_recovery_to_healthy`: 0.35 -> 0.30, debt 0.0035 -> 0.003.
- `test_degraded_penalty_floor` comment: 0.35 -> 0.30.
- `test_degraded_state_with_failures` comment: 0.35 -> 0.30, debt 0.29 -> 0.25.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
